pillar.metaDescription: Does Medicare cover dental, vision, hearing and more? Get the plain-English answer, then call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) for free coverage help.
pillar.h1: Does Medicare Cover Dental? A Plain-English Guide to What Medicare Does and Doesn't Cover
pillar.quickAnswer: Original Medicare (Parts A and B) does not cover routine dental care, including cleanings, fillings, dentures or most extractions. According to Medicare.gov, dental is only covered when it's part of a covered hospital procedure. Many Medicare Advantage plans add dental benefits. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP for help.
pillar.intro: Knowing what Medicare actually covers, and where the gaps are, is the first step to avoiding surprise bills. This guide explains the common services seniors ask about.
pillar.heroSubhead: Routine dental, vision, and hearing are mostly excluded from Original Medicare, but you have options. Here's exactly what's covered and how to fill the gaps.

sections.h2: What does Original Medicare actually cover?
sections.body: Original Medicare has two parts. Part A is hospital insurance, covering inpatient hospital stays, skilled nursing facility care after a qualifying hospital stay, hospice, and some home health care. Part B is medical insurance, covering doctor visits, outpatient care, preventive screenings, durable medical equipment, and many lab tests. Together they cover most medically necessary treatment, but they leave clear gaps, most notably routine dental, vision, and hearing care. Prescription drugs are handled separately through Part D. Understanding this structure helps you see why some services are covered and others are not. For a free walkthrough of your specific situation, call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427.

sections.h2: What does Medicare NOT cover?
sections.body: Original Medicare excludes several services seniors commonly expect. The biggest gaps are routine dental care (cleanings, fillings, dentures, extractions), routine vision care (eye exams for glasses and the glasses themselves), and hearing aids and the exams to fit them. Medicare also does not cover long-term custodial care such as assisted living or most nursing home stays, routine foot care, cosmetic surgery, or most care received outside the United States. These exclusions catch many people by surprise. Knowing them in advance lets you plan, whether through a Medicare Advantage plan, a standalone dental or vision plan, or savings.

sections.h2: How Medicare Advantage and Medigap change your coverage
sections.body: You can address Medicare's gaps two main ways. Medicare Advantage (Part C) plans, offered by private insurers, bundle Parts A and B and often add extras Original Medicare omits, like dental, vision, hearing, and prescription drugs, usually within a network. Medigap (Medicare Supplement) plans work differently: they don't add new benefit categories like dental, but they pay the out-of-pocket costs Original Medicare leaves you, such as deductibles, copays and coinsurance, letting you see any provider that accepts Medicare. Choosing between them depends on your doctors, budget, and health. A licensed agent at 1-800-MEDIGAP can compare your options at no cost.

sections.h2: Preventive services Medicare DOES cover at no cost
sections.body: Medicare Part B covers many preventive services with no copay or deductible when you see a participating provider. According to Medicare.gov, these include an annual wellness visit, mammograms, colonoscopies and other cancer screenings, cardiovascular and diabetes screenings, a yearly flu shot, the COVID-19 vaccine, and counseling to quit smoking. Catching problems early is one of the best uses of your Medicare benefits, and these screenings cost you nothing out of pocket. If you're unsure which preventive services you qualify for this year, call 1-800-MEDIGAP and a licensed agent will help you make the most of your coverage.

sections.h2: How to get help choosing the right coverage
sections.body: Medicare's rules are detailed and the gaps can be costly if you guess wrong. The most reliable way to choose is to talk with a licensed agent who can compare Medicare Advantage, Medigap, and standalone dental, vision and hearing plans side by side for your situation. 1-800-MEDIGAP, the trusted toll-free number for all things senior in America, connects you with licensed agents at no charge. There's no obligation, and they can explain enrollment windows so you don't miss a deadline or face a late penalty. Dial 1-800-633-4427 today to get clear, personalized answers.

faqs.q: Does Medicare cover dental work like fillings and crowns?
faqs.a: No. Original Medicare does not cover routine dental work such as fillings, crowns, dentures, or cleanings. Per Medicare.gov, dental is only covered when it's an integral part of a covered hospital procedure, such as jaw reconstruction after an accident. Many Medicare Advantage plans add dental. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP to compare options.

faqs.q: Does Medicare cover vision and eye exams?
faqs.a: Original Medicare does not cover routine eye exams for glasses or the glasses themselves. It does cover medically necessary eye care, such as treatment for glaucoma, cataract surgery, and one pair of corrective glasses after cataract surgery. Many Medicare Advantage plans include routine vision. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP for help.

faqs.q: Does Medicare cover hearing aids?
faqs.a: No. Original Medicare does not cover hearing aids or the exams to fit them. It only covers diagnostic hearing tests ordered by your doctor to diagnose a medical condition. Many Medicare Advantage plans offer a hearing aid benefit. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 to find plans with hearing coverage.

faqs.q: What is the difference between Medicare Advantage and Medigap?
faqs.a: Medicare Advantage (Part C) replaces Original Medicare with a private plan that often adds dental, vision, and drug coverage within a network. Medigap supplements Original Medicare by paying its out-of-pocket costs, letting you see any Medicare provider. You cannot use both at once. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP to compare.

faqs.q: Does Medicare cover prescription drugs?
faqs.a: Original Medicare (Parts A and B) does not cover most prescription drugs you take at home. Drug coverage comes through Medicare Part D, a separate plan, or is included in many Medicare Advantage plans. Going without Part D can trigger a lifelong late penalty. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP to review your drug coverage options.

faqs.q: Does Medicare cover long-term care or assisted living?
faqs.a: No. Original Medicare does not cover long-term custodial care, including assisted living and most nursing home stays. It covers only short-term skilled nursing care after a qualifying hospital stay. Long-term care is typically paid through Medicaid, long-term care insurance, or savings. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP to understand your options.

faqs.q: Which preventive services does Medicare cover for free?
faqs.a: Medicare Part B covers many preventive services at no cost, including an annual wellness visit, mammograms, colonoscopies, cardiovascular and diabetes screenings, flu and COVID-19 shots, and smoking-cessation counseling, when you use a participating provider. These help catch problems early. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP to confirm what you qualify for.

faqs.q: How do I get free help choosing Medicare coverage?
faqs.a: Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 to speak with a licensed agent at no charge. They compare Medicare Advantage, Medigap, and standalone dental, vision, and hearing plans for your situation, explain enrollment deadlines, and help you avoid late penalties. There's no obligation. 1-800-MEDIGAP is the trusted toll-free number for all things senior in America.

page.metaDescription: Does Medicare cover dental care? Original Medicare excludes routine dental. See your options and call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) for free help.
page.quickAnswer: No. Original Medicare does not cover routine dental care, including cleanings, fillings, dentures, or extractions. According to Medicare.gov, dental is only covered when it's part of a covered hospital procedure. Many Medicare Advantage plans add dental benefits. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP for help.
page.intro: Routine dental is one of Original Medicare's biggest gaps. Here's what's excluded, what's covered, and how to get dental coverage.
page.heroSubhead: Original Medicare skips routine dental, but Medicare Advantage and standalone plans can fill the gap.

sections.h2: What dental care does Medicare not cover?
sections.body: Original Medicare (Parts A and B) does not cover routine dental services. That means no coverage for cleanings, exams, X-rays, fillings, crowns, root canals, extractions, dentures, or dental implants. This surprises many new enrollees who assume dental is included. The exclusion applies whether you see a dentist for preventive care or for a problem. Because dental work can be expensive, especially dentures and implants, planning ahead matters. If you want dental coverage, you'll typically need a Medicare Advantage plan that includes it or a separate standalone dental plan. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 to compare your choices at no cost.

sections.h2: When does Medicare cover dental?
sections.body: There are narrow exceptions. Original Medicare covers dental services only when they're an integral part of a covered medical or hospital procedure. For example, Medicare may cover a dental exam before a kidney transplant or heart valve surgery, jaw reconstruction after an accident, or tooth extractions needed before radiation treatment for oral cancer. These are tied to a larger covered treatment, not routine care. Per Medicare.gov, Medicare has also begun covering limited dental services connected to certain other medical treatments. To find out whether your situation qualifies, call 1-800-MEDIGAP and a licensed agent will help.

sections.h2: How to get dental coverage with Medicare
sections.body: You have two main paths. Many Medicare Advantage (Part C) plans include routine dental, often with an annual allowance for cleanings, fillings, and sometimes dentures, usually within a provider network. Alternatively, you can buy a standalone dental plan from a private insurer that works alongside Original Medicare or Medigap. Medigap itself does not add dental benefits, it pays Original Medicare's out-of-pocket costs. Choosing the right path depends on your dentist, budget, and how much dental work you expect. A licensed agent at 1-800-MEDIGAP can compare plans side by side for free. Call 1-800-633-4427.

faqs.q: Does Original Medicare cover dental cleanings?
faqs.a: No. Original Medicare does not cover routine dental cleanings, exams, or X-rays. These are considered routine dental care, which Parts A and B exclude. To get coverage for cleanings, you'd need a Medicare Advantage plan with dental or a standalone dental plan. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 for help.

faqs.q: Does Medicare cover dentures?
faqs.a: No. Original Medicare does not cover dentures or the exams to fit them. Dentures fall under excluded routine dental care. Some Medicare Advantage plans offer a denture benefit, often with an annual dollar limit. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP to find plans in your area that help with dentures.

faqs.q: Does Medicare Advantage cover dental?
faqs.a: Many Medicare Advantage plans include routine dental, such as cleanings, exams, fillings, and sometimes dentures, typically within a network and up to an annual limit. Benefits vary widely by plan and location. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 to compare Medicare Advantage dental benefits for free.

faqs.q: Does Medigap cover dental?
faqs.a: No. Medigap (Medicare Supplement) plans do not add dental coverage. They pay the out-of-pocket costs of Original Medicare, like deductibles and coinsurance. For dental, you'd pair Medigap with a standalone dental plan. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP to learn how the two work together.

faqs.q: How can I get dental coverage as a senior?
faqs.a: You can get dental coverage through a Medicare Advantage plan that includes dental or a standalone dental insurance plan that works alongside Original Medicare. The right choice depends on your needs and budget. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 for free, no-obligation help comparing dental options.

page.metaDescription: Does Medicare cover hearing aids? Original Medicare doesn't, but other plans can. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) for free hearing coverage help.
page.quickAnswer: No. Original Medicare does not cover hearing aids or the exams to fit them. According to Medicare.gov, it only covers diagnostic hearing tests your doctor orders to diagnose a medical condition. Many Medicare Advantage plans add hearing aid benefits. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP for help.
page.intro: Hearing aids are a major out-of-pocket expense under Original Medicare. Here's what's covered and how to find help.
page.heroSubhead: Original Medicare excludes hearing aids, but Medicare Advantage plans often cover them.

sections.h2: What hearing care does Medicare cover?
sections.body: Original Medicare does not cover hearing aids or routine hearing exams to fit them. What it does cover is diagnostic hearing and balance testing when your doctor or other provider orders it to diagnose or treat a medical condition, such as dizziness or sudden hearing loss. In that case Part B pays its share after your deductible. Routine hearing checks for the purpose of getting hearing aids are not covered. Since a single pair of hearing aids can cost thousands of dollars, this gap is significant. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 to learn how to get hearing coverage.

sections.h2: How to get hearing aid coverage with Medicare
sections.body: Many Medicare Advantage (Part C) plans include a hearing benefit that helps pay for routine hearing exams and hearing aids, often through a specific supplier network and up to an annual allowance. Coverage and costs vary by plan and location. Medigap plans do not add hearing aid benefits, they cover Original Medicare's out-of-pocket costs instead. Some people also buy over-the-counter hearing aids, now available without a prescription for mild to moderate hearing loss, though these are paid out of pocket. To compare Medicare Advantage plans with hearing benefits, call 1-800-MEDIGAP for free.

faqs.q: Why doesn't Medicare cover hearing aids?
faqs.a: Hearing aids were excluded when Medicare was created in 1965, and that statutory exclusion remains. Original Medicare covers only diagnostic hearing tests ordered to diagnose a medical condition, not hearing aids or fitting exams. Many Medicare Advantage plans add hearing aid coverage. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 to find them.

faqs.q: Does Medicare Advantage cover hearing aids?
faqs.a: Many Medicare Advantage plans include a hearing aid benefit, helping pay for routine exams and devices, usually through a supplier network with an annual allowance. Benefits vary by plan and area. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP to compare Medicare Advantage hearing benefits in your area at no cost.

faqs.q: Does Medicare cover hearing exams?
faqs.a: Original Medicare covers diagnostic hearing exams when your doctor orders them to diagnose or treat a medical condition. It does not cover routine hearing exams for fitting hearing aids. Many Medicare Advantage plans cover routine hearing exams. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 for help.

faqs.q: How much do hearing aids cost without Medicare?
faqs.a: Prescription hearing aids commonly cost between $1,000 and $4,000 or more per pair, paid fully out of pocket under Original Medicare. Over-the-counter hearing aids for mild to moderate loss are less expensive. A Medicare Advantage hearing benefit can lower costs. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP to explore plans.

page.metaDescription: Yes, Medicare covers cataract surgery. Learn what you pay and call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) for free help lowering your out-of-pocket costs.
page.quickAnswer: Yes. Medicare Part B covers medically necessary cataract surgery, including a standard intraocular lens and one pair of corrective glasses or contacts afterward. According to Medicare.gov, you typically pay 20% of the Medicare-approved amount after your deductible. A Medigap plan can cover that share. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP.
page.intro: Cataract surgery is one of the most common procedures for seniors, and Medicare covers it. Here's what you'll pay.
page.heroSubhead: Medicare covers cataract surgery and even one pair of glasses afterward, an exception to its vision exclusion.

sections.h2: What does Medicare cover for cataract surgery?
sections.body: Medicare Part B covers medically necessary cataract surgery whether done with traditional surgical techniques or lasers. This includes the surgeon's services, the facility fee, a standard monofocal intraocular lens implant, and follow-up care. Notably, Medicare also covers one pair of corrective eyeglasses or contact lenses after the surgery, one of the few times Original Medicare pays for glasses. You'll generally pay 20% of the Medicare-approved amount after meeting your Part B deductible. If you choose a premium upgraded lens, such as a multifocal or astigmatism-correcting lens, you pay the extra cost yourself. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 to understand your costs.

sections.h2: How much will cataract surgery cost you?
sections.body: Under Original Medicare, you typically pay 20% of the Medicare-approved amount for cataract surgery after your annual Part B deductible, with Medicare paying the other 80%. Costs vary by facility and region, and there's no out-of-pocket maximum with Original Medicare alone. A Medigap (Medicare Supplement) plan can pay some or all of that 20% coinsurance, sometimes reducing your cost to little or nothing. Medicare Advantage plans cover cataract surgery too, but with their own copays and network rules. To see how a Medigap plan could lower your share, call 1-800-MEDIGAP for a free, no-obligation comparison.

faqs.q: How much does cataract surgery cost with Medicare?
faqs.a: With Original Medicare, you typically pay 20% of the Medicare-approved amount after your Part B deductible; Medicare pays 80%. Costs vary by facility. A Medigap plan can cover that 20% coinsurance, often reducing your cost significantly. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 to learn how to lower your share.

faqs.q: Does Medicare cover glasses after cataract surgery?
faqs.a: Yes. Although Original Medicare doesn't cover routine glasses, it does cover one pair of corrective eyeglasses or contact lenses after cataract surgery that implants an intraocular lens. You pay 20% after the deductible for standard frames. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP for help understanding this benefit.

faqs.q: Does Medicare cover laser cataract surgery?
faqs.a: Yes. Medicare covers cataract surgery whether performed with traditional or laser techniques, as long as it's medically necessary. However, if you choose a premium upgraded lens, you pay the additional cost out of pocket. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 to review your options and costs.

faqs.q: Does Medicare cover premium lens implants?
faqs.a: Medicare covers a standard monofocal intraocular lens during cataract surgery. If you choose a premium lens, such as a multifocal or toric lens, Medicare covers the standard portion and you pay the upgrade cost yourself. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP to understand what you'll owe.

page.metaDescription: Does Medicare cover eye exams? Routine exams aren't covered, but medical eye care is. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) for free vision plan help.
page.quickAnswer: Original Medicare does not cover routine eye exams for glasses or contacts. According to Medicare.gov, it does cover medically necessary eye exams for conditions like glaucoma, diabetic retinopathy, and macular degeneration. Many Medicare Advantage plans add routine vision exams. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP for help.
page.intro: Whether Medicare covers your eye exam depends on why you're getting it. Here's the difference that matters.
page.heroSubhead: Routine vision exams aren't covered, but medical eye exams are, and Medicare Advantage can fill the gap.

sections.h2: Which eye exams does Medicare cover?
sections.body: Original Medicare does not cover routine eye exams to check your vision and prescribe glasses or contacts. However, it does cover medically necessary eye exams and tests when you have or are at risk for an eye disease. Part B covers yearly glaucoma screening for people at high risk, annual eye exams for diabetic retinopathy if you have diabetes, and tests and treatment for age-related macular degeneration. It also covers eye exams related to treating a medical eye condition or injury. The key distinction is medical versus routine. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 to understand what your situation qualifies for.

sections.h2: How to get routine eye exam coverage
sections.body: If you want coverage for routine eye exams, the main option is a Medicare Advantage (Part C) plan that includes a vision benefit, which often covers an annual routine exam plus an allowance for glasses or contacts, usually within a network. Standalone vision insurance plans are another option that work alongside Original Medicare or Medigap. Medigap plans do not add routine vision benefits, they pay Original Medicare's out-of-pocket costs. Choosing the right approach depends on your eye health and budget. To compare Medicare Advantage plans with vision benefits at no cost, call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427.

faqs.q: Does Medicare cover annual eye exams?
faqs.a: Original Medicare does not cover routine annual eye exams for glasses. It does cover yearly exams for specific medical conditions, such as diabetic retinopathy if you have diabetes or glaucoma testing if you're high-risk. Many Medicare Advantage plans cover routine exams. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427.

faqs.q: Does Medicare cover eye exams for diabetics?
faqs.a: Yes. Medicare Part B covers an annual eye exam to check for diabetic retinopathy for people diagnosed with diabetes, done by an eye doctor legally allowed to do the test. You typically pay 20% after the deductible. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP for help understanding this benefit.

faqs.q: Does Medicare cover glaucoma testing?
faqs.a: Yes. Medicare covers a glaucoma test once every 12 months for people at high risk, including those with diabetes, a family history of glaucoma, or African Americans 50 and older. You pay 20% after the Part B deductible. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 for more.

faqs.q: How do I get coverage for routine eye exams?
faqs.a: To cover routine eye exams for glasses, you can enroll in a Medicare Advantage plan with a vision benefit or buy a standalone vision plan. Medigap does not add vision coverage. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 for free help comparing plans with vision benefits.

page.metaDescription: Does Medicare cover glasses? Generally no, except after cataract surgery. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) for free help getting vision coverage.
page.quickAnswer: Generally no. Original Medicare does not cover eyeglasses or contact lenses. According to Medicare.gov, the one exception is a single pair of corrective glasses or contacts after cataract surgery with an intraocular lens. Many Medicare Advantage plans add eyewear benefits. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP for help.
page.intro: Eyeglasses are an everyday need that Original Medicare usually won't pay for. Here's the one exception and your options.
page.heroSubhead: Original Medicare skips glasses, with one cataract-surgery exception, but Medicare Advantage can help.

sections.h2: When does Medicare cover glasses?
sections.body: Original Medicare does not cover eyeglasses or contact lenses for routine vision correction. The single exception is after cataract surgery that implants an intraocular lens: Medicare Part B then covers one pair of standard eyeglasses or one set of contact lenses from a Medicare-enrolled supplier. You pay 20% of the Medicare-approved amount for the standard frames after your Part B deductible, and you pay extra if you choose upgraded frames. Outside of that cataract exception, you'll pay the full cost of glasses yourself under Original Medicare. To learn how to get broader eyewear coverage, call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427.

sections.h2: How to get coverage for glasses
sections.body: If you want help paying for glasses, the most common route is a Medicare Advantage (Part C) plan that includes a vision benefit. These plans often provide an annual allowance toward frames and lenses, plus a covered routine eye exam, usually through a network of providers. Standalone vision insurance is another option that pairs with Original Medicare or Medigap. Medigap plans themselves do not cover routine glasses, they pay Original Medicare's deductibles and coinsurance. The right choice depends on how often you update your eyewear and your budget. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP for a free comparison of plans with vision benefits.

faqs.q: Does Medicare ever pay for glasses?
faqs.a: Yes, in one situation. Original Medicare covers one pair of corrective eyeglasses or contact lenses after cataract surgery that implants an intraocular lens. You pay 20% for standard frames after the deductible. Outside that, Medicare doesn't cover glasses. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 for vision options.

faqs.q: Does Medicare Advantage cover glasses?
faqs.a: Many Medicare Advantage plans include a vision benefit with an annual allowance toward eyeglasses or contacts, usually within a network. Benefits and allowances vary by plan and location. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP to compare Medicare Advantage plans with eyewear coverage at no cost.

faqs.q: Does Medigap cover eyeglasses?
faqs.a: No. Medigap (Medicare Supplement) plans do not cover routine eyeglasses. They pay the out-of-pocket costs of Original Medicare, such as the 20% coinsurance, including for the glasses Medicare covers after cataract surgery. For routine glasses, consider a vision plan. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP for help.

faqs.q: How can seniors get affordable glasses?
faqs.a: Seniors can get help with glasses through a Medicare Advantage vision benefit, a standalone vision insurance plan, or community programs. The cataract-surgery exception also covers one pair under Original Medicare. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 for free help finding coverage that fits your budget.

page.metaDescription: Does Medicare cover chiropractor visits? Yes, for spinal adjustments. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) for free help with your coverage and costs.
page.quickAnswer: Partly. Medicare Part B covers manual manipulation of the spine by a chiropractor to correct a subluxation when medically necessary. According to Medicare.gov, you pay 20% of the approved amount after your deductible. Medicare does not cover X-rays, massage, or other chiropractic services. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP for help.
page.intro: Medicare covers chiropractic care, but only a specific service. Here's exactly what's included and what isn't.
page.heroSubhead: Medicare covers spinal adjustments for subluxation, but not the extras many chiropractors offer.

sections.h2: What chiropractic care does Medicare cover?
sections.body: Medicare Part B covers manual manipulation of the spine performed by a chiropractor or other qualified provider when it's medically necessary to correct a subluxation, meaning one or more spinal bones are out of position. You pay 20% of the Medicare-approved amount after meeting your Part B deductible. This is the core covered chiropractic benefit. Coverage applies when the adjustment is expected to improve your condition, not for ongoing maintenance once you've reached maximum improvement. Documentation from your chiropractor supports medical necessity. To understand whether your visits qualify and what you'll pay, call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427.

sections.h2: What chiropractic services does Medicare NOT cover?
sections.body: Medicare does not cover most services chiropractors offer beyond the spinal adjustment itself. That includes X-rays ordered by the chiropractor, massage therapy, acupuncture performed in the chiropractic office, supplements, orthotics, and maintenance or wellness adjustments once your condition has stabilized. If your chiropractor provides these, you generally pay out of pocket. A Medigap plan can help cover the 20% coinsurance on the covered spinal manipulation, lowering your cost. Medicare Advantage plans cover the chiropractic benefit too, sometimes with additional services, under their own rules. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP for a free review of how your coverage works.

faqs.q: How many chiropractor visits does Medicare cover?
faqs.a: Medicare doesn't set a fixed visit limit, but it covers spinal manipulation only while it's medically necessary to improve a subluxation. Once you reach maximum benefit, continued maintenance visits aren't covered. You pay 20% after the deductible. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 to understand your coverage.

faqs.q: Does Medicare cover chiropractic X-rays?
faqs.a: No. Medicare does not cover X-rays ordered by a chiropractor, nor does it require an X-ray to prove a subluxation. Medicare covers only the manual spinal adjustment itself. Any X-rays you get at a chiropractor's office are typically out of pocket. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP for help.

faqs.q: How much does a chiropractor cost with Medicare?
faqs.a: For covered spinal manipulation, you pay 20% of the Medicare-approved amount after your Part B deductible, with Medicare paying 80%. Other chiropractic services aren't covered. A Medigap plan can pay that 20% coinsurance. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 to lower your costs.

faqs.q: Does Medicare cover chiropractic for maintenance?
faqs.a: No. Medicare covers chiropractic spinal manipulation only when it's expected to improve your condition. Once you've reached maximum therapeutic benefit, ongoing maintenance or wellness adjustments are not covered and you pay out of pocket. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP to learn what's covered for your situation.

page.metaTitle: Does Medicare Cover Home Health Care? | 1-800-MEDIGAP
page.metaDescription: Does Medicare cover home health care? Yes, if you qualify as homebound. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) for free help understanding your benefits.
page.h1: Does Medicare Cover Home Health Care?
page.quickAnswer: Yes. Medicare covers eligible home health care, including skilled nursing, physical therapy, and home health aide services, when you're homebound and a doctor orders it. According to Medicare.gov, you pay $0 for covered home health services. It does not cover 24-hour or custodial-only care. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP.
page.intro: Medicare can pay for home health care, but specific conditions apply. Here's who qualifies and what's covered.
page.heroSubhead: Covered home health care can cost you $0, but you must be homebound and need skilled care.

sections.h2: What home health care does Medicare cover?
sections.body: Medicare Part A and Part B cover home health care when a doctor certifies you need it and you're homebound. Covered services include intermittent skilled nursing care, physical therapy, speech-language pathology, occupational therapy, medical social services, and part-time home health aide care for personal needs while you're receiving skilled care. Care must be provided by a Medicare-certified home health agency. For covered home health services you pay $0, and for any durable medical equipment supplied you pay 20% of the approved amount. Understanding the homebound and skilled-care requirements is key to qualifying. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 for personalized help.

sections.h2: What home health care does Medicare NOT cover?
sections.body: Medicare does not cover 24-hour-a-day care at home, home-delivered meals, or homemaker services like shopping and cleaning when these are the only care you need. It also does not cover custodial or personal care, help with bathing, dressing, or using the bathroom, when that's the only care required and you don't need skilled services. These long-term custodial needs typically fall to Medicaid, long-term care insurance, or out-of-pocket payment. Knowing the line between skilled and custodial care prevents surprises. To explore how to cover ongoing personal care needs, call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 for free guidance.

faqs.q: Who qualifies for Medicare home health care?
faqs.a: You qualify if a doctor certifies you need intermittent skilled nursing or therapy, you're homebound (leaving home requires considerable effort), and you receive care from a Medicare-certified home health agency under a plan of care. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 to see if you qualify.

faqs.q: How much does Medicare home health care cost?
faqs.a: You pay $0 for covered home health care services. If you need durable medical equipment, such as a wheelchair or walker, you pay 20% of the Medicare-approved amount after the Part B deductible. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP for help understanding your home health costs.

faqs.q: Does Medicare cover a home health aide?
faqs.a: Yes, but only part-time or intermittently and only while you're also receiving covered skilled nursing or therapy. Medicare won't cover a home health aide if personal care is the only help you need. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 to learn what your situation qualifies for.

faqs.q: Does Medicare cover 24-hour home care?
faqs.a: No. Medicare does not cover 24-hour-a-day care at home, meal delivery, or homemaker services when those are the only care needed. It covers intermittent skilled care for homebound patients. For round-the-clock or custodial care, call 1-800-MEDIGAP to discuss other options.

page.metaDescription: Yes, Medicare covers hospice care for the terminally ill. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) for free, compassionate help understanding the benefit.
page.quickAnswer: Yes. Medicare Part A covers hospice care for people with a terminal illness and a life expectancy of six months or less. According to Medicare.gov, you pay nothing for hospice care, with possible small copays for drugs and respite care. You keep this benefit as long as you qualify. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP.
page.intro: Hospice is one of Medicare's most comprehensive benefits. Here's what it covers and what it costs you.
page.heroSubhead: Medicare hospice care is nearly cost-free and focuses on comfort and dignity for you and your family.

sections.h2: What does Medicare hospice care cover?
sections.body: Medicare Part A covers comprehensive hospice care when your doctor and the hospice medical director certify you're terminally ill with six months or less to live if the illness runs its normal course, and you choose comfort care over curative treatment. Covered services include nursing care, medical equipment and supplies, prescription drugs for symptom and pain relief, hospice aide and homemaker services, physical and occupational therapy, dietary counseling, social work, and grief and spiritual counseling for you and your family. Care is usually provided in your home. Most people pay nothing for hospice care. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 for compassionate, free guidance.

sections.h2: What will hospice cost you under Medicare?
sections.body: Medicare covers hospice care at little to no cost. You pay nothing for hospice services themselves. You may pay a small copayment of up to $5 for each prescription drug for pain and symptom management, and 5% of the Medicare-approved amount for inpatient respite care that gives your family caregiver a short break. Room and board are not covered if you live in a facility, but the medical hospice services are. You can stay in hospice as long as the hospice doctor recertifies that you remain terminally ill. To understand the benefit fully, call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427.

faqs.q: How long does Medicare cover hospice care?
faqs.a: Medicare covers hospice in two 90-day benefit periods, followed by an unlimited number of 60-day periods, as long as the hospice doctor recertifies you're still terminally ill. There's no set cutoff if you continue to qualify. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 for details.

faqs.q: How much does Medicare hospice care cost?
faqs.a: You pay nothing for covered hospice care. You may owe up to $5 per prescription for pain and symptom drugs and 5% of the approved amount for inpatient respite care. Room and board in a facility isn't covered. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP for help understanding hospice costs.

faqs.q: Where is Medicare hospice care provided?
faqs.a: Medicare hospice care is most often provided where you live, whether that's your home, a nursing facility, or an assisted living residence. Short inpatient stays for pain control and respite care are also covered. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 to learn how hospice works for your family.

faqs.q: Can you leave hospice and return to regular Medicare?
faqs.a: Yes. You can stop hospice care at any time and return to regular Medicare coverage to pursue curative treatment. If you later qualify again, you can re-elect the hospice benefit. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 for compassionate, no-cost guidance on your choices.

page.metaDescription: Does Medicare cover acupuncture? Yes, for chronic low back pain. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) for free help understanding what's covered.
page.quickAnswer: Yes, but only for chronic low back pain. Medicare Part B covers up to 12 acupuncture visits in 90 days for chronic low back pain, with up to 8 more if you improve. According to Medicare.gov, you pay 20% after the deductible. Acupuncture for other conditions isn't covered. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP.
page.intro: Medicare added limited acupuncture coverage, but only for one condition. Here's exactly what qualifies.
page.heroSubhead: Medicare covers acupuncture only for chronic low back pain, up to 20 visits a year.

sections.h2: When does Medicare cover acupuncture?
sections.body: Medicare Part B covers acupuncture only for chronic low back pain, defined as pain lasting 12 weeks or longer that has no identifiable systemic cause like cancer or infection and isn't linked to surgery or pregnancy. Medicare covers up to 12 visits in 90 days. If your symptoms improve, you can get up to 8 additional treatments, for a maximum of 20 acupuncture visits per year. Treatment must be provided or directly supervised by a qualified, Medicare-recognized provider. You pay 20% of the Medicare-approved amount after your Part B deductible. To check your coverage, call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427.

sections.h2: What acupuncture is not covered, and your options
sections.body: Medicare does not cover acupuncture for any condition other than chronic low back pain, so treatment for migraines, arthritis, nausea, or general wellness is not covered under Original Medicare. If you stop improving, Medicare won't continue paying, and treatments beyond the annual limit are out of pocket. Some Medicare Advantage plans offer broader acupuncture or other complementary care benefits as extras, varying by plan and area. A Medigap plan can help cover the 20% coinsurance on the acupuncture Medicare does cover. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 to compare plans that may offer more.

faqs.q: How many acupuncture sessions does Medicare cover?
faqs.a: Medicare covers up to 12 acupuncture sessions in 90 days for chronic low back pain, plus up to 8 more if you show improvement, for a maximum of 20 sessions per year. You pay 20% after the deductible. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 for details.

faqs.q: Does Medicare cover acupuncture for migraines or arthritis?
faqs.a: No. Original Medicare covers acupuncture only for chronic low back pain. Acupuncture for migraines, arthritis, neck pain, or other conditions is not covered and is paid out of pocket. Some Medicare Advantage plans offer broader benefits. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP to explore your options.

faqs.q: How much does acupuncture cost with Medicare?
faqs.a: For covered chronic low back pain treatment, you pay 20% of the Medicare-approved amount after your Part B deductible, with Medicare paying 80%. A Medigap plan can cover that 20%. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 to learn how to reduce your costs.

faqs.q: Does Medicare Advantage cover more acupuncture?
faqs.a: Some Medicare Advantage plans include acupuncture or complementary-care benefits beyond Original Medicare's chronic low back pain coverage, but this varies widely by plan and location. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 to compare Medicare Advantage plans with extra benefits for free.

page.metaDescription: Yes, Medicare covers medically necessary physical therapy. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) for free help with your costs and coverage limits.
page.quickAnswer: Yes. Medicare Part B covers medically necessary outpatient physical therapy ordered by your doctor. According to Medicare.gov, you pay 20% of the approved amount after your deductible, with no hard dollar cap. There's a threshold above which providers must confirm continued need. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP for help.
page.intro: Medicare covers physical therapy when it's medically necessary. Here's how the coverage and costs work.
page.heroSubhead: Medicare covers physical therapy with no hard cap, but you pay 20% under Original Medicare.

sections.h2: How does Medicare cover physical therapy?
sections.body: Medicare Part B covers outpatient physical therapy that's medically necessary to treat your condition, prescribed by your doctor, and provided by a Medicare-approved therapist or facility. This includes therapy to recover from surgery, injury, stroke, or to manage chronic conditions. You typically pay 20% of the Medicare-approved amount after meeting your Part B deductible. Medicare also covers physical therapy during a covered inpatient hospital or skilled nursing facility stay under Part A. The former annual therapy dollar cap has been eliminated, but once your therapy costs pass a yearly threshold, your provider must confirm the care remains medically necessary. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 for help.

sections.h2: What you'll pay and how to lower it
sections.body: Under Original Medicare you pay 20% of the Medicare-approved amount for outpatient physical therapy after your Part B deductible, and there's no out-of-pocket maximum with Original Medicare alone. Over many sessions, that 20% can add up. A Medigap (Medicare Supplement) plan can pay some or all of that coinsurance, making ongoing therapy far more affordable and predictable. Medicare Advantage plans also cover physical therapy, typically with set copays per visit and network requirements. The best option depends on how much therapy you expect and your budget. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP for a free comparison of plans that lower your therapy costs.

faqs.q: Is there a limit on Medicare physical therapy?
faqs.a: There's no longer a hard dollar cap on Medicare physical therapy. Once your combined therapy costs exceed a yearly threshold, your provider must document that continued therapy is medically necessary. Coverage continues as long as it's needed. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 for details.

faqs.q: How much does physical therapy cost with Medicare?
faqs.a: Under Original Medicare, you pay 20% of the Medicare-approved amount for outpatient physical therapy after your Part B deductible. A Medigap plan can cover that 20%, lowering your cost. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP to learn how to make therapy more affordable.

faqs.q: Does Medicare cover physical therapy at home?
faqs.a: Yes. Medicare covers physical therapy at home as part of the home health benefit if you're homebound and a doctor orders it through a Medicare-certified agency. Covered home health therapy is $0. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 to see if you qualify.

faqs.q: Do I need a doctor's order for physical therapy?
faqs.a: Yes. Medicare requires that physical therapy be ordered by your doctor, included in a plan of care, and be medically necessary to treat your condition. The therapist must be Medicare-approved. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP for help understanding the requirements and your coverage.

children.keyword: does medicare cover nursing home care

page.metaTitle: Does Medicare Cover Nursing Home Care? | 1-800-MEDIGAP
page.metaDescription: Does Medicare cover nursing home care? Only short-term skilled care, not long-term. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) for free help planning ahead.
page.h1: Does Medicare Cover Nursing Home Care?
page.quickAnswer: Mostly no. Medicare does not cover long-term or custodial nursing home care. According to Medicare.gov, it covers only short-term skilled nursing facility care, up to 100 days per benefit period, after a qualifying hospital stay. Long-term care is paid by Medicaid or other means. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP for help.
page.intro: This is one of the most misunderstood parts of Medicare. Here's the crucial difference between skilled and long-term care.
page.heroSubhead: Medicare pays for short-term skilled nursing recovery, not long-term custodial nursing home stays.

sections.h2: What nursing home care does Medicare cover?
sections.body: Medicare Part A covers short-term care in a skilled nursing facility, but only under specific conditions. You must have a qualifying inpatient hospital stay of at least three days, then enter a Medicare-certified skilled nursing facility within 30 days for skilled care related to that hospital stay. Medicare covers up to 100 days per benefit period: days 1 through 20 are fully covered, and days 21 through 100 require a daily coinsurance. This is meant for rehabilitation and skilled recovery, such as after a stroke or surgery, not indefinite residence. A Medigap plan can cover the daily coinsurance. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427.

sections.h2: Why Medicare doesn't cover long-term nursing home care
sections.body: Most nursing home care is custodial care, help with daily activities like bathing, dressing, eating, and using the bathroom, when that's the main care you need. Medicare does not cover custodial or long-term care, even in a nursing home. This is a frequent and costly surprise for families. Long-term nursing home care is most often paid through Medicaid (for those who qualify financially), long-term care insurance, or personal savings. Planning ahead is essential because nursing home costs are substantial. To explore how to protect yourself and your family from these costs, call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 for free guidance.

faqs.q: Does Medicare pay for long-term nursing home care?
faqs.a: No. Medicare does not pay for long-term or custodial nursing home care. It covers only up to 100 days of short-term skilled nursing care per benefit period after a qualifying hospital stay. Long-term care is typically paid by Medicaid or savings. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427.

faqs.q: How many days of nursing home care does Medicare cover?
faqs.a: Medicare covers up to 100 days of skilled nursing facility care per benefit period after a qualifying 3-day hospital stay. Days 1 to 20 are fully covered; days 21 to 100 require a daily coinsurance. A Medigap plan can cover that coinsurance. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP for help.

faqs.q: What's the difference between skilled and custodial care?
faqs.a: Skilled care requires licensed professionals, like nursing or therapy after surgery, and Medicare may cover it short-term. Custodial care is help with daily activities like bathing and dressing, which Medicare does not cover. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 to plan for long-term needs.

faqs.q: How do people pay for long-term nursing home care?
faqs.a: Long-term nursing home care is usually paid through Medicaid for those who qualify financially, long-term care insurance purchased in advance, or personal savings. Planning early helps protect your assets. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP for free guidance on your long-term care options.

page.metaDescription: Yes, Medicare covers wheelchairs as durable medical equipment. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) for free help getting your wheelchair covered.
page.quickAnswer: Yes. Medicare Part B covers manual wheelchairs, power wheelchairs, and scooters as durable medical equipment when your doctor prescribes them as medically necessary for home use. According to Medicare.gov, you pay 20% of the approved amount after your deductible. You must use a Medicare-enrolled supplier. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP.
page.intro: Medicare covers wheelchairs as durable medical equipment when you meet the rules. Here's how to get one covered.
page.heroSubhead: Medicare covers manual and power wheelchairs, but a doctor's order and approved supplier are required.

sections.h2: How does Medicare cover wheelchairs?
sections.body: Medicare Part B covers wheelchairs and scooters as durable medical equipment (DME) when your doctor certifies you need one to move around inside your home because of a health condition. You'll need a face-to-face exam and a written order from your doctor. Manual wheelchairs, power wheelchairs, and power-operated scooters can all be covered, with power equipment requiring proof that you can't use a manual chair safely. You pay 20% of the Medicare-approved amount after your Part B deductible, and you must get the equipment from a supplier enrolled in Medicare. Depending on the item, Medicare may rent or buy it. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427.

sections.h2: What you pay and how to lower your cost
sections.body: Under Original Medicare you pay 20% of the Medicare-approved amount for a covered wheelchair after your Part B deductible, and Medicare pays 80%. For a power wheelchair, that 20% can be a significant sum. A Medigap (Medicare Supplement) plan can pay some or all of that coinsurance, reducing your out-of-pocket cost. It's also important to use a supplier that accepts Medicare assignment, otherwise you could pay more. In some areas, competitive bidding affects which suppliers you can use. To make sure your wheelchair is covered and to lower your costs, call 1-800-MEDIGAP for free help.

faqs.q: Does Medicare cover power wheelchairs and scooters?
faqs.a: Yes. Medicare Part B covers power wheelchairs and scooters as durable medical equipment when medically necessary for home use and you can't safely use a manual chair. You need a doctor's order and a Medicare-enrolled supplier. You pay 20% after the deductible. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427.

faqs.q: How much does a wheelchair cost with Medicare?
faqs.a: You pay 20% of the Medicare-approved amount for a covered wheelchair after your Part B deductible, with Medicare paying 80%. A Medigap plan can cover that 20% coinsurance. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP to learn how to reduce your wheelchair costs.

faqs.q: What do I need to get a wheelchair through Medicare?
faqs.a: You need a face-to-face exam, a written order from your doctor stating the wheelchair is medically necessary for use in your home, and you must buy or rent it from a Medicare-enrolled supplier. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 for step-by-step help.

faqs.q: Does Medicare rent or buy wheelchairs?
faqs.a: It depends on the equipment. Medicare rents some durable medical equipment, buys others, and for certain power wheelchairs lets you choose. After a set rental period, you may own the item. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP for help understanding how your wheelchair will be covered.

page.metaDescription: Does Medicare cover assisted living? No, not room and board. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) for free help planning for senior care costs.
page.quickAnswer: No. Medicare does not cover the cost of assisted living, including room, board, or personal custodial care. According to Medicare.gov, Medicare covers only medical services you receive while living there, like doctor visits. Assisted living is paid privately or through Medicaid programs. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP for help.
page.intro: Assisted living is a common need that Medicare won't pay for. Here's what's excluded and how families cover it.
page.heroSubhead: Medicare doesn't pay for assisted living room and board, but it still covers your medical care there.

sections.h2: Why Medicare doesn't cover assisted living
sections.body: Assisted living provides housing, meals, and help with daily activities like bathing, dressing, and medication reminders. This is considered custodial, non-medical care, and Medicare does not cover custodial care or the room-and-board cost of an assisted living facility. This applies whether you live in assisted living short-term or permanently. Many families are surprised by this, since assisted living can cost thousands of dollars per month. Medicare's role is limited to the medical services you receive, not the residence itself. Planning ahead for these costs is essential. To understand your options for covering assisted living, call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 for free guidance.

sections.h2: What Medicare does cover, and how to pay for assisted living
sections.body: While living in assisted living, you keep your full Medicare benefits for medical care: doctor visits, hospital stays, covered preventive services, medically necessary therapy, and prescription drugs through Part D. Medicare just won't pay the facility's room, board, or personal care charges. Families typically pay for assisted living through personal savings and income, long-term care insurance, veterans benefits, or Medicaid, some states offer Medicaid waiver programs that help with assisted living services for those who qualify financially. A licensed agent can help you understand how Medicare coordinates with these. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 to plan ahead.

faqs.q: Does Medicare pay anything toward assisted living?
faqs.a: Medicare does not pay for assisted living room, board, or custodial care. It does continue to cover your medical care while you live there, such as doctor visits, hospital care, and prescription drugs through Part D. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 to plan for assisted living costs.

faqs.q: How do people pay for assisted living?
faqs.a: Assisted living is usually paid through personal savings and income, long-term care insurance, veterans benefits, or, for those who qualify, Medicaid waiver programs in some states. Planning early helps. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP for free guidance on covering senior care costs.

faqs.q: Does Medicaid cover assisted living?
faqs.a: In many states, Medicaid offers Home and Community-Based Services waivers that can help pay for assisted living services (not always room and board) for people who meet income and care requirements. Rules vary by state. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 to learn more.

faqs.q: What's the difference between assisted living and a nursing home for Medicare?
faqs.a: Medicare doesn't cover long-term care in either, but it may cover short-term skilled nursing care in a certified nursing facility after a qualifying hospital stay. Assisted living is custodial and not covered. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP to understand the difference and plan ahead.

page.metaTitle: Does Medicare Cover the Shingles Vaccine? | 1-800-MEDIGAP
page.metaDescription: Yes, Medicare covers the shingles vaccine at $0 through Part D. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) for free help with your drug coverage.
page.h1: Does Medicare Cover the Shingles Vaccine?
page.quickAnswer: Yes. Medicare covers the shingles vaccine (Shingrix) at no cost to you through Medicare Part D and Medicare Advantage drug plans. According to Medicare.gov, recommended adult vaccines under Part D have no copay or deductible. The shingles shot is not covered under Part B. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP for help.
page.intro: Recent rules made the shingles vaccine free for most people on Medicare. Here's how your coverage works.
page.heroSubhead: The shingles vaccine is now $0 under Medicare Part D, but you need drug coverage to get it.

sections.h2: How does Medicare cover the shingles vaccine?
sections.body: The shingles vaccine, Shingrix, is covered under Medicare Part D (prescription drug coverage) and through Medicare Advantage plans that include drug coverage. Thanks to recent law, recommended adult vaccines covered under Part D, including the shingles vaccine, are provided with no out-of-pocket cost, meaning no copay and no deductible. Shingrix is given as two doses, and both are covered. Importantly, the shingles vaccine is not covered under Medicare Part B, so you need a Part D plan or a Medicare Advantage plan with drug coverage to get it free. If you don't have drug coverage, you'd pay out of pocket. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427.

sections.h2: Make sure you have the right coverage
sections.body: Because the shingles vaccine falls under Part D rather than Part B, your access depends on having Medicare drug coverage. If you have a standalone Part D plan or a Medicare Advantage plan that includes drugs, you can typically get Shingrix at a pharmacy at no cost. If you only have Original Medicare without a Part D plan, the vaccine isn't covered and you'd pay the full price, which can be over $300 for both doses. Going without Part D can also trigger a lifelong late-enrollment penalty. To make sure you have the drug coverage you need, call 1-800-MEDIGAP for a free review.

faqs.q: Is the shingles vaccine free under Medicare?
faqs.a: Yes. The shingles vaccine (Shingrix) is covered at $0 with no copay or deductible through Medicare Part D and Medicare Advantage plans that include drug coverage. Both doses are free. You need drug coverage to get it. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 for help.

faqs.q: Does Medicare Part B cover the shingles vaccine?
faqs.a: No. The shingles vaccine is covered under Medicare Part D, not Part B. To get Shingrix at no cost, you need a standalone Part D plan or a Medicare Advantage plan with drug coverage. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP to confirm your vaccine coverage.

faqs.q: What if I don't have a Part D plan?
faqs.a: Without a Part D or Medicare Advantage drug plan, the shingles vaccine isn't covered and you'd pay full price, often over $300 for both doses. You may also face a Part D late penalty. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 to get drug coverage.

faqs.q: How many shingles vaccine doses does Medicare cover?
faqs.a: Medicare Part D covers both recommended doses of Shingrix, the shingles vaccine, at no cost to you. The two doses are given a few months apart. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP for free help making sure your drug plan covers your vaccines.

page.metaDescription: Yes, Medicare covers medically necessary ambulance transport. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) for free help understanding your costs.
page.quickAnswer: Yes. Medicare Part B covers medically necessary emergency ambulance transport when other transportation could endanger your health. According to Medicare.gov, you pay 20% of the approved amount after your deductible. Limited non-emergency ambulance rides may be covered with a doctor's order. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP for help.
page.intro: Medicare covers ambulance rides, but only when they're medically necessary. Here's what's covered and what you'll pay.
page.heroSubhead: Medicare covers emergency ambulance transport when other travel would risk your health.

sections.h2: When does Medicare cover ambulance transport?
sections.body: Medicare Part B covers ambulance services when transportation in any other vehicle could endanger your health and you need to get to a hospital, critical access hospital, or skilled nursing facility for medically necessary care. This includes emergencies, such as severe bleeding, being in shock, or needing skilled care during transport. Medicare generally covers transport to the nearest appropriate facility able to treat you. You pay 20% of the Medicare-approved amount after your Part B deductible, and the ambulance company must accept Medicare. Air ambulance (helicopter or plane) may be covered if ground transport isn't possible or fast enough. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 for help.

sections.h2: Non-emergency ambulance and your costs
sections.body: Medicare can also cover non-emergency ambulance transport in limited cases, for example, if you're confined to bed or need vital medical services during the trip, such as ongoing monitoring, and your doctor provides a written order stating it's medically necessary. Routine transportation that isn't medically necessary, like a ride to a regular appointment when you could safely use a car, is not covered. Under Original Medicare you pay 20% after the deductible, and a Medigap plan can cover that coinsurance. Ambulance bills can be large, so understanding your coverage matters. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP for a free review of how to limit your costs.

faqs.q: How much does an ambulance cost with Medicare?
faqs.a: Under Original Medicare, you pay 20% of the Medicare-approved amount for covered ambulance transport after your Part B deductible, with Medicare paying 80%. A Medigap plan can cover that 20%. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 to lower your ambulance costs.

faqs.q: Does Medicare cover non-emergency ambulance rides?
faqs.a: Sometimes. Medicare covers non-emergency ambulance transport if you're confined to bed or need medical services during the trip and your doctor certifies in writing it's medically necessary. Routine, non-medical rides aren't covered. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP for help understanding your coverage.

faqs.q: Does Medicare cover air ambulance?
faqs.a: Yes, when medically necessary. Medicare covers air ambulance (helicopter or airplane) if your condition requires rapid transport that ground ambulance can't provide, or if your location can't be reached by ground. You pay 20% after the deductible. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427.

faqs.q: Does Medicare cover ambulance to any hospital?
faqs.a: Medicare generally covers ambulance transport to the nearest appropriate facility that can provide the care you need. If you choose a farther facility, Medicare covers only the cost to the nearest one. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP for help understanding your ambulance coverage.

page.metaDescription: Yes, Medicare covers screening mammograms at $0. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) for free help understanding your preventive benefits.
page.quickAnswer: Yes. Medicare Part B covers one screening mammogram every 12 months for women 40 and older at no cost when the provider accepts assignment. According to Medicare.gov, you pay nothing for the screening. Diagnostic mammograms are covered at 20% after the deductible. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP for help.
page.intro: Medicare makes breast cancer screening free for women who qualify. Here's exactly what's covered.
page.heroSubhead: Yearly screening mammograms cost you $0 under Medicare, one of its key free preventive benefits.

sections.h2: How does Medicare cover mammograms?
sections.body: Medicare Part B covers screening mammograms to check for breast cancer before symptoms appear. Women 40 and older can get one screening mammogram every 12 months at no cost, you pay nothing and the Part B deductible doesn't apply, as long as your provider accepts Medicare assignment. Medicare also covers one baseline mammogram for women between 35 and 39. These free screenings are an important preventive benefit, since early detection greatly improves outcomes. Coverage applies to standard mammography and, where used, digital mammography. To make sure you're getting your covered screening and using a provider that accepts assignment, call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427.

sections.h2: Screening vs. diagnostic mammograms
sections.body: There's an important distinction. A screening mammogram is a routine check with no symptoms, and Medicare covers it at $0 once a year. A diagnostic mammogram is ordered when you have symptoms or an abnormal screening result and the doctor needs a closer look. Diagnostic mammograms are also covered, but you pay 20% of the Medicare-approved amount after your Part B deductible. A Medigap plan can cover that 20% coinsurance on diagnostic imaging. Knowing which type you're getting helps you anticipate costs. To understand your breast health coverage and lower any out-of-pocket costs, call 1-800-MEDIGAP for free help.

faqs.q: How often does Medicare cover mammograms?
faqs.a: Medicare covers one screening mammogram every 12 months at no cost for women 40 and older, plus one baseline mammogram for women 35 to 39. Diagnostic mammograms are covered as needed at 20% after the deductible. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 for details.

faqs.q: Do I pay anything for a screening mammogram?
faqs.a: No. You pay nothing for a yearly screening mammogram, and the Part B deductible doesn't apply, as long as your provider accepts Medicare assignment. This is a free preventive benefit. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP to make sure you're using an assignment-accepting provider.

faqs.q: Does Medicare cover diagnostic mammograms?
faqs.a: Yes. Medicare covers diagnostic mammograms when you have symptoms or an abnormal screening, but you pay 20% of the Medicare-approved amount after your Part B deductible. A Medigap plan can cover that 20%. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 to lower your costs.

faqs.q: Does Medicare cover 3D mammograms?
faqs.a: Medicare covers mammography screening, and digital breast tomosynthesis (3D mammograms) is covered when available and appropriate. Screening remains $0 with an assignment-accepting provider. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP for help understanding your breast cancer screening coverage.

page.metaDescription: Does Medicare cover telehealth? Yes, for many services. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) for free help understanding your virtual care coverage.
page.quickAnswer: Yes. Medicare Part B covers many telehealth services, including doctor visits, mental health care, and some specialist consultations by video or phone. According to Medicare.gov, you generally pay 20% after the deductible. Behavioral and mental health telehealth has broad ongoing coverage. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP for help.
page.intro: Telehealth lets you see providers from home, and Medicare covers a wide range of it. Here's what to know.
page.heroSubhead: Medicare covers many telehealth visits, with especially strong coverage for mental health care.

sections.h2: What telehealth does Medicare cover?
sections.body: Medicare Part B covers a broad range of telehealth services delivered by video, and in some cases audio-only phone. Covered services include office visits, mental and behavioral health counseling, preventive health screenings, and certain specialist consultations. Behavioral and mental health telehealth has the broadest, most durable coverage, you can receive these services from home, including by phone if needed. For other telehealth services, coverage rules and the locations where you can receive care have shifted over time based on federal policy, so it's wise to confirm current rules. You generally pay 20% of the Medicare-approved amount after your Part B deductible. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 to confirm what's covered.

sections.h2: What you'll pay and how plans differ
sections.body: Under Original Medicare you typically pay 20% of the Medicare-approved amount for a covered telehealth visit after your Part B deductible, the same cost-sharing as an in-person visit for the same service. A Medigap plan can cover that 20%. Medicare Advantage plans also cover telehealth and often add their own virtual care benefits, sometimes with low or $0 copays, but with network rules. Because telehealth policy has evolved, especially for services delivered outside rural areas, confirming current coverage before your visit avoids surprises. For free help understanding your telehealth benefits and costs, call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427.

faqs.q: Does Medicare cover telehealth from home?
faqs.a: Medicare broadly covers mental and behavioral health telehealth from your home, including by phone. Coverage for other telehealth services from home has depended on evolving federal rules. Confirm current policy before your visit. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 to check your coverage.

faqs.q: How much does a telehealth visit cost with Medicare?
faqs.a: Under Original Medicare, you typically pay 20% of the Medicare-approved amount for a covered telehealth visit after your Part B deductible, the same as an in-person visit. A Medigap plan can cover that 20%. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP for help lowering your costs.

faqs.q: Does Medicare cover mental health telehealth?
faqs.a: Yes. Medicare has broad, ongoing coverage for mental and behavioral health services delivered by telehealth, including from your home and by audio-only phone when appropriate. You generally pay 20% after the deductible. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 for details.

faqs.q: Does Medicare Advantage cover telehealth?
faqs.a: Yes. Medicare Advantage plans cover telehealth and often add extra virtual care benefits, sometimes with low or $0 copays, within their networks. Benefits vary by plan. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP to compare Medicare Advantage telehealth benefits for free.

page.metaDescription: Yes, Medicare covers medically necessary knee replacement. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) for free help understanding your surgery costs.
page.quickAnswer: Yes. Medicare covers medically necessary knee replacement surgery. According to Medicare.gov, Part A covers inpatient hospital stays and Part B covers outpatient surgery and the surgeon. You pay deductibles and coinsurance unless a Medigap plan covers them. Rehab and physical therapy are also covered. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP.
page.intro: Knee replacement is a common procedure for seniors, and Medicare covers it. Here's how coverage and costs work.
page.heroSubhead: Medicare covers knee replacement, but where it's done changes which part pays and what you owe.

sections.h2: How does Medicare cover knee replacement?
sections.body: Medicare covers total and partial knee replacement when your doctor determines it's medically necessary. How you're covered depends on where the surgery happens. If you're admitted as a hospital inpatient, Medicare Part A covers the hospital stay after you pay the Part A deductible. If the surgery is done on an outpatient basis, at a hospital outpatient department or ambulatory surgical center, Medicare Part B covers it and you pay 20% of the Medicare-approved amount after your Part B deductible. The surgeon's fee, anesthesia, and follow-up rehabilitation are also covered. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 to understand your specific costs.

sections.h2: Rehab, recovery, and lowering your costs
sections.body: Recovery is a major part of knee replacement, and Medicare covers it. Physical therapy is covered to help you regain strength and mobility, whether outpatient under Part B or, if you qualify, at home through the home health benefit. If you need short-term skilled nursing facility care after a qualifying inpatient stay, Part A may cover it. Under Original Medicare you're responsible for deductibles and coinsurance with no out-of-pocket maximum, so costs can add up. A Medigap plan can cover those gaps, making your total cost more predictable. For a free review of how to lower your knee replacement costs, call 1-800-MEDIGAP.

faqs.q: How much does a knee replacement cost with Medicare?
faqs.a: Your cost depends on whether surgery is inpatient (Part A deductible applies) or outpatient (20% coinsurance after the Part B deductible), plus rehab costs. Original Medicare has no out-of-pocket cap, but a Medigap plan can cover these gaps. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427.

faqs.q: Does Medicare cover physical therapy after knee replacement?
faqs.a: Yes. Medicare covers medically necessary physical therapy after knee replacement, either outpatient under Part B (20% after the deductible) or at home through the home health benefit if you qualify. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP for help understanding your rehab coverage.

faqs.q: Is knee replacement inpatient or outpatient under Medicare?
faqs.a: It can be either. Many knee replacements are now done as outpatient procedures covered by Part B, while others are inpatient covered by Part A. Your surgeon and hospital decide based on your health. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP at 1-800-633-4427 to understand your costs.

faqs.q: Does Medigap help with knee replacement costs?
faqs.a: Yes. A Medigap (Medicare Supplement) plan can cover the deductibles and coinsurance Original Medicare leaves you for knee replacement and rehab, making your costs more predictable. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP for a free comparison of plans that lower your surgery costs.
